ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-15-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via Zoom, for and with Lisa Brinkman, Conservator/Trustee; Caroline Hinshaw, via Zoom, court appointed counsel, for Conservatee.

The court acknowledges Ms. Brinkman's efforts with the related Conservatorship and Trust matters and accordingly believes that the request to increase hourly fees from $45 to $50 is acceptable. Ms. Hinshaw advises that she agrees with the court's position regarding the request to increase Ms. Brinkman's hourly fees. The court, Ms. Brinkman, and Ms. Callejo discuss the $1,000 that the Conservatee currently receives per month with Ms. Brinkman reporting how the money is used. Both Ms. Brinkman and Ms. Callejo believe that the $1,000 per month is high and that it could be lowered to $500 per month. The court summarizes the preceding and confirms with the parties that as of Oct-01-2022, the court will order that the Conservatee's household allowance is to be reduced to $500 per month.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-14-297991 Conservatorship of Louise Tschudi and
PTR-19-303197 The Rolf Tschudi and Louise Tschudi 1989 Trust.

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo appeared by zoom for and with Lisa Brinkman (Conservator/Trustee) by zoom.
Caroline Hinshaw (Court appointed Counsel) appeared by zoom for Louise Tschudi (Conservatee/Settlor).

With respect to the conservatorship matter, Ms. Hinshaw stated that she believes she has seen everything that was filed, that she got the documents from Ms. Callejo and that she got a copy of the trust from the Court's website. The Court questions why the reverse mortgage is not listed as a liability of the trust, the Conservator hiring family without permission from the Court/payment to Mary Brinkmann again without prior Court approval, the need for Ron Kapp's transportation services/care and when the Conservatee was no longer able to leave her home, etc. Ms. Callejo stated that she does not know why the reverse mortgage was not listed as a lability of the trust, that they gave the information to the Accountant and this is how the accounting was prepared, and that there were no payments to Mary Brinkman in 2019/2020. Ms. Callejo also stated that Ms. Tschudi wants to stay in her home, the Ms. Tschudi's health has declined, that Mary Brinkman filled in the gaps during emergencies, that the Conservatee now requires 24-hour care and that her Client is okay with being charged the $57.00 unexplained item. Ms. Brinkman stated that Ron's care and transportation services were needed when Ms. Tschudi needed to go to Kaiser, that it was easier for him to drop Ms. Tschudi off and wait for her, that it was safer and cost effective, that Ms. Tschudi hasn't left the house since COVID and that Ms. Tschudi has not been able to leave her home in the last six months. Ms. Brinkman also stated that the Conservatee still gets around her house just fine, that she looks good, that the house is clean, and that Ms. Tschudi is happy. The Court informed Ms. Callejo that the other unsupported items may result in a relatively modest cut in hours. Ms. Callejo stated that extensive research was done regarding reverse mortgages, that the result was a very good loan with a low interest rate and that it saved money. Ms. Hinshaw stated that the Conservatee is extremely fortunate to have Ms. Brinkman and Mr. Kapp, that they are longtime friends and spoke of the good effort they have made in the Conservatee's care. The Third Account and Report of Successor Conservator, etc. and the First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
